mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising or held at restrictive levels, equity discount rates stay elevated and the present value of expected cash flows falls, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days are negative. The signal is strongest when inflation is still elevated, because policy restraint is more likely to persist.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the average forward return is non-negative for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in retail sales growth signals stronger household demand and revenue momentum, which lifts expected earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is a trend signal rather than a one-day reaction and should appear when demand improves faster than prior months.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS growth does not correspond to higher mean 20d SPX returns, or if the rank correlation between RSAFS changes and forward returns stays below 0.03 in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above a short-run threshold marks a deterioration in labor-market momentum that weakens earnings expectations and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect should be concentrated when UNRATE has already turned up from recent lows.
failure_condition: Reject if, after the trigger is met, the next 5-day SPX return is positive in a majority of cases or the average return is not below the unconditional sample mean by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and housing starts signals broad real-activity contraction, tighter earnings expectations, and falling cyclical equity valuations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The interaction matters because weak output alone is less informative than synchronized weakness across goods production and construction.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-INDPRO/weak-HOUST condition does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 20d returns, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts from depressed levels indicates stabilization in construction demand and a broader improvement in cyclical confidence, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is slow-moving because it works through earnings revisions and risk sentiment rather than immediate price discovery.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month housing-start rebounds are not followed by above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the hit rate is below 55% over the out-of-sample sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is still elevated and the policy rate is above neutral, equity markets remain vulnerable because tighter real financing conditions compress valuation multiples and raise recession odds. Under this combination, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 days should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-inflation/high-rate regime does not produce negative average 20d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is statistically indistinguishable from zero in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid slowdown in payroll growth signals that firms are cutting labor demand, which typically precedes weaker consumer spending and lower equity earnings expectations. The signal should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when payroll gains fall below recent trend.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if payroll slowdowns do not precede negative 20d SPX returns more often than chance, or if the average forward return after the trigger is non-negative over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with rising employment indicates a broad demand-led expansion that supports earnings breadth and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when GDP growth is weak or payrolls stop improving, because then the expansion is not broad enough to lift equities.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-and-payroll expansion condition does not deliver positive 60d SPX returns above the unconditional average, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.
